MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Defendants' Answer And Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
JOSHUA A. BASKIN, ESQ. (415) 947-2000, WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI FOR DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., MINYI XU.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. Counsel indicates he does not contest the tentative ruling but only seeks clarification. The Court informs Counsel he cannot have an ex parte communication since Counsel failed to advise the other side of his appearance this morning. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC. and MINYI XU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Defendants' Answer And Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Defendants' "motion for leave to amend defendants' answer and leave to file cross-complaint" is granted.

"That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP SEC 428.50(c).) A proposed amended pleading's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

The same liberal principles apply to cross-complaints. (Thompson Pacific Construction, Inc. v. City of Sunnyvale (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 525, 543-45 [defendant obtains new information "a few weeks before trial"].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
